__comment: MIRROR OF: pipeline/v2/rubric.py — keep in sync

specificity.description: does the script reference the user's specific intent verbatim or near-verbatim?

anchors.10: Quotes the user's intent in the first 15 seconds, references the first_action by the closing
anchors.7: References the intent paraphrased or partially
anchors.4: Mentions the general task but doesn't quote the user's specific words
anchors.1: Generic motivation, could be the same script for any user

command_density.description: how many imperative verbs vs descriptive sentences?

anchors.10: 5+ imperative verbs, each on its own line, no rhetorical questions
anchors.7: 3-4 imperatives, mix of commands and statements
anchors.1: No commands, all rhetorical or descriptive

structural_arc_completeness.description: does the script hit all 5 beats (groggy reality, quoted intent, concrete hero, grounding phrase, command)?

anchors.10: All 5 beats present, in order, distinct
anchors.4: 3 of 5 beats present or out of order

cliche_freedom.description: absence of subtle motivational clichés (beyond the banned list)

anchors.10: Zero clichés, every line earns its place
anchors.7: 1-2 mild clichés that still feel earned
anchors.4: Multiple subtle clichés, generic in places

hero_anchor_concreteness.description: is the hero referenced with specific habit/time/place, or vague platitude?

anchors.10: Specific time + place + habit (e.g., '4am at the Lakers facility, before anyone else')
anchors.7: Specific habit, vague on time or place
anchors.1: No hero reference or invented quote

voice_directness.description: statements and commands vs questions and hedges

anchors.10: All declaratives and imperatives, zero question marks, zero 'you should' or 'try to'
